items.title: Leaders condemn 'disgusting' disruption by 'cretins' at WA Anzac Day services

children.content: Leaders have condemned "cretins" who interjected and booed at Perth and Western Australian Anzac Day ceremonies, with the premier labelling their actions "disgusting and disrespectful".

People with scarves gather for an Anzac ceremony

items.title: Valerie the sausage dog comparisons as lost pup turns up alive months later

children.content: A dachshund puppy is reunited with its owner after being lost for almost three months, in an adventure spanning multiple suburbs.

gallery.altText: A dog looks up at a camera playing in plants

thumbnail.altText: A dog looks up at a camera playing in plants

items.title: AFL sacks appeals boss after homophobia statement labelled 'disgusting'

children.content: Will Houghton KC is sacked as the chair of the AFL appeals board after the body's widely criticised statement and decision in the case of St Kilda player Lance Collard.

image.altText: The AFL logo on the side of a building, partially obscured by a tree branch.

items.title: Horticultural production losses reach $25 million after cyclone

children.content: Growers across Western Australia's Gascoyne take a huge financial hit after four natural disasters in the first four months of 2026.

image.altText: close up of damaged bananas after cyclone narelle

items.title: Perth woman warns about pedicure safety after severe infection

children.content: Kyla Willcox says she almost lost her toe from a potentially fatal infection after having a pedicure at a Perth nail salon.

gallery.altText: Foot with big toe covered in white bandage

thumbnail.altText: Foot with big toe covered in white bandage

items.title: AI traffic fines set to net $440m in driver fines across Perth and WA

image.altText: A scan of an infringement notice

children.content: The cameras have handed out around 184,000 infringements since they started issuing fines in October last year, according to the WA government.

items.title: Sniper who killed top bikie in public execution revealed after gag on identity lifted

children.content: The former soldier who publicly assassinated ex-Rebels bikie boss Nick Martin at Perth Motorplex in 2020 can be publicly identified for the first time, after he successfully overturned a court order suppressing his identity.

image.altText: A smiling man wearing a cap and white shirt on the right poses in a selfie with a quokka on the left.

items.title: Woodside tells inquiry no projects 'would survive' with additional tax

children.content: Oil and gas giant Woodside denies its business model is "weak" while arguing that paying a proposed flat tax on gas exports would kill the company's projects.

image.altText: Seven members of a Senate committee sitting at a bench during a parliamentary inquiry hearing.

items.title: Extra rangers at Karijini campsites after three dingo attacks within a week

children.content: A camper describes a "brazen" dingo attack on a four-year-old girl in northern Western Australia as officers continue to search for three aggressive dingoes.

articles.title: Three children attacked by dingoes in national park

children.content: Three children have been attacked by dingoes in Karijini National Park since Saturday, prompting a warning from authorities.

image.altText: A dingo howling towards the camera

gallery.altText: A dingo in green and yellow shrubs looks at the camera with its tongue out.
A sign saying do not feed dingoes.

A sign saying do not feed dingoes.

items.title: Dozen more possible human burial sites found at Rottnest Island church

children.content: Authorities believe there may be a dozen more unmarked Aboriginal burial sites near where human remains were found on Western Australia's tourist haven Rottnest Island earlier this month.

image.altText: A look over rock groynes at boats in a bay at Rottnest with a lighthouse in the background.

items.title: 'Stressful': Huge fuel price tag of family's 800km round-trip for state training

children.content: Sports clubs and competitions are reporting lower participation, as players withdraw from training and competition due to high fuel and travel costs.

image.altText: Lois Garner sits down next to an indoor basketball court.

items.title: From the WA bush to the jungle of Vietnam

children.content: Yamatji man Graham Taylor was just 19 when he shipped off to Vietnam. More than 50 years on, the brotherhood forged in the jungle of Vietnam remains solid.

items.title: NDIS cuts a 'slap in the face, punch in the guts' for those needing care in regional Australia

children.content: People living with disability in regional areas already face extra hurdles in accessing support and families fear changes to the NDIS could have irreversible consequences.

image.altText: A young boy and girl both wearing glasses

items.title: 'We're not ready to leave': Inside the messy dynamic of a no-grounds eviction

children.content: Evicting a tenant without cause offers landlords a simple way out, but the issue can be much more complex than it appears. One such example of that complexity is Eleanor Kahlo's case of her no-grounds eviction.

Eleanor in the drivers seat of a van, looking out her side window.

items.title: 'Not a nice place to live at the moment': Kununurra residents at wits' end

children.content: Stolen cars and accommodation break-ins have led to growing frustration in Western Australia's north.

image.altText: A couple stand looking sternly with a car behind them

items.title: Alleged threat to ‘blow up’ Perth Airport terminal sparks chaos, lands man in court

children.content: A man who allegedly threatened to "blow up" Perth Airport's main international terminal, forcing its evacuation and causing traveller chaos, has appeared in court.

items.title: Fight over legal costs from Gina Rinehart judgement begins

children.content: A legal wrangle is underway over what costs mining businesswoman Gina Rinehart is liable for following last week's judgment in her epic court battle with the family of her father's former business partner.

image.altText: Gina Rinehart smiles while standing in front of cattle pens

items.title: 'Safer' digital drivers' licences to be rolled out in WA

children.content: The WA government allocates $28 million for the roll-out of digital drivers' licences in the state, estimated to be implemented by the end of next year.

image.altText: A woman holds a phone with a driver licence on the screen
